content:
Case 18:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 34
- Sex: female
- Chief complaint: Follow-up for transfusion-dependent pancytopenia with persistent severe neutropenia and sequelae of disseminated fusariosis
- Vital signs: Temp 37.7 °C, BP 104/66 mmHg, HR 102/min (regular), RR 18/min, SpO₂ 94% on room air.
Setting: Outpatient hematology follow-up in a patient with longstanding PNH/aplastic marrow failure and recent disseminated fungal infection
Capabilities: You are seeing a profoundly cytopenic, heavily transfusion-dependent patient in the outpatient clinic. You can review symptoms, exam, current medications, cytopenia pattern, and recent cross-sectional imaging. You decide on urgency and scope of inpatient versus outpatient diagnostics and whether to proceed to repeat bone marrow evaluation and extended infectious work-up.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "complete blood count with differential and recent transfusion history"}}

response.request: complete blood count with differential and recent transfusion history

content:
Cbc Chemistry:
CBC: Hb 7.8 g/dL, WBC 0.9 × 10⁹/L, ANC 0.15 × 10⁹/L, Plt 18 × 10⁹/L; MCV 95 fL, MCH 31 pg.
Differential: neutrophils 11%, lymphocytes 64%, monocytes 19%, eosinophils 4%, basophils 2%.
Reticulocytes: 12 × 10⁹/L.
Coagulation: aPTT 35 sec, INR 1.3.
Chemistry: Na 134 mmol/L, K 4.6 mmol/L, creatinine 1.4 mg/dL, BUN 28 mg/dL, AST 41 U/L, ALT 44 U/L, bilirubin 1.6 mg/dL, LDH 328 U/L, uric acid 6.2 mg/dL, CRP 10.8 mg/dL, haptoglobin 21 mg/dL, ferritin 4950 ng/mL.
Immunology: CD4⁺ 380/µL, CD8⁺ 590/µL (CD4/CD8 ratio 0.64). Immunoglobulins: IgG 840 mg/dL (normal), IgA 68 mg/dL, IgM 42 mg/dL.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "current medication list and recent antifungal therapy details"}}

response.request: current medication list and recent antifungal therapy details

content:
History Physical:
2024-09-15: The patient presented for follow-up with increasing transfusion requirements, requiring RBC transfusions weekly and platelet transfusions up to 3–4 times per week. ANC remained critically low at 0.1–0.2 × 10⁹/L. She reported skin lesions that showed gradual healing with postinflammatory hyperpigmentation and impaired vision with persistent blurriness and reduced acuity. She reported intermittent cough with occasional blood-streaked sputum and chronic nasal congestion.
Her general condition was poor with pronounced fatigue, weakness, and emotional distress. She appeared tearful and anxious during consultation, expressing significant fear of renewed long hospital stays, remembering the prolonged summer admission for disseminated fusariosis. ECOG performance status was 1.
Current medication: pegcetacoplan 1080 mg s.c. twice weekly, eltrombopag 150 mg 1-0-0, ciclosporin A 150 mg 1-0-1, voriconazole 200 mg 1-0-1, terbinafine 250 mg 1-0-0, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-1, cotrimoxazole 960 mg 1-0-0 (Mo/Wed/Fri), sitagliptin 100 mg 1-0-0, pregabalin 75 mg 1-0-1, sertraline 50 mg 1-0-0, pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, filgrastim 30 Mio IU s.c. 2–3×/week as needed, epoetin alfa 40,000 IU s.c. once weekly, lorazepam 1 mg on demand for anxiety or insomnia.
Vital signs: temperature 37.7 °C, blood pressure 104/66 mmHg, heart rate 102/min (regular), respiratory rate 18/min, oxygen saturation 94% on room air.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "recent cross-sectional imaging (chest/abdomen CT or MRI) for evaluation of residual fungal disease and organ involvement"}}

response.request: recent cross-sectional imaging (chest/abdomen CT or MRI) for evaluation of residual fungal disease and organ involvement

content:
Baseline Imaging:
2024-09-18 Abdominal ultrasound: Normal-sized liver (14.8 cm) with homogeneous echotexture, no focal lesions. Intra- and extrahepatic bile ducts not dilated (DHC 5 mm). Gallbladder poorly visualized (postprandial). Spleen 11.2 × 4.6 cm, regular margins, no focal lesions. Kidneys normal in size and morphology (left 10.8 cm, right 11.1 cm) with preserved corticomedullary differentiation. No hydronephrosis, no lymphadenopathy, no ascites.
2024-09-18 Echocardiogram: Sinus rhythm, preserved LV ejection fraction (55%), normal RV function, no relevant valvular disease or pericardial effusion.
2024-09-19 CT of the paranasal sinuses: Mucosal thickening in both maxillary sinuses (right > left) and ethmoidal cells, consistent with chronic fungal sinusitis. No fluid levels, bone erosion, or orbital involvement.
2024-09-20 CT thorax: Multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules (1–1.5 cm), partly cavitary with halo sign, consistent with residual pulmonary fungal lesions. No pleural effusion or mediastinal lymphadenopathy.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "ophthalmologic examination details for vision impairment and any evidence of fungal eye involvement"}}

response.request: ophthalmologic examination details for vision impairment and any evidence of fungal eye involvement

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Transfusion-dependent PNH with aplastic marrow failure, persistent severe neutropenia (ANC 0.15 \u00d7 10\u2079/L), and residual disseminated fusariosis with pulmonary nodules and chronic fungal sinusitis", "differentials": ["Progressive PNH with aplastic transformation", "Persistent invasive fungal infection despite antifungal therapy", "Drug-induced cytopenias (immunosuppressants)", "Secondary hemophagocytic syndrome"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Continue IV voriconazole or convert to IV liposomal amphotericin B for residual pulmonary fusariosis given persistent cavitary nodules", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Continue filgrastim (G-CSF) at increased frequency (daily) to address severe neutropenia", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Consider eltrombopag dose optimization and romiplostim addition for platelet support", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Repeat bone marrow evaluation to assess marrow cellularity and rule out evolutive disease/secondary MDS", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "CT-guided biopsy of pulmonary nodule for histopathology and fungal culture to guide antifungal therapy", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Inpatient admission for IV antifungal therapy intensification and neutropenic fever monitoring", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Ophthalmology evaluation urgently for visual symptoms to rule out fungal eye involvement/orbital extension", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Transfusion support (RBC weekly, platelets 3-4x/week) as currently prescribed", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Consider eculizumab or ravulizumab if not already on adequate complement inhibition for PNH hemolysis", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Transfusion-dependent PNH with aplastic marrow failure, persistent severe neutropenia (ANC 0.15 × 10⁹/L), and residual disseminated fusariosis with pulmonary nodules and chronic fungal sinusitis
differentials: Persistent invasive fungal infection despite antifungal therapy

treatment_plan.action: Continue IV voriconazole or convert to IV liposomal amphotericin B for residual pulmonary fusariosis given persistent cavitary nodules
Continue filgrastim (G-CSF) at increased frequency (daily) to address severe neutropenia
Consider eltrombopag dose optimization and romiplostim addition for platelet support
Repeat bone marrow evaluation to assess marrow cellularity and rule out evolutive disease/secondary MDS
CT-guided biopsy of pulmonary nodule for histopathology and fungal culture to guide antifungal therapy
Inpatient admission for IV antifungal therapy intensification and neutropenic fever monitoring
Ophthalmology evaluation urgently for visual symptoms to rule out fungal eye involvement/orbital extension
Transfusion support (RBC weekly, platelets 3-4x/week) as currently prescribed
Consider eculizumab or ravulizumab if not already on adequate complement inhibition for PNH hemolysis

Round 2 begins.
Setting: Post–bone marrow work-up visit with new cytogenetic data
Capabilities: You now have bone marrow morphology, flow cytometry, histology, and karyotype results. You can integrate hypocellular marrow findings, monosomy 7, and the known PNH clone, and reassess the diagnosis and risk profile. You decide whether standard immunosuppression remains appropriate or whether clonal evolution mandates a change in strategy and transplant planning.
Request additional information or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow morphology, flow cytometry, histology, and karyotype results"}}

response.request: bone marrow morphology, flow cytometry, histology, and karyotype results

content:
Bone Marrow:
2024-09-22: Bone marrow aspiration showed marked reduction of granulopoiesis and virtually absent megakaryopoiesis. Erythropoiesis was markedly reduced with dysplastic features including nuclear irregularities, megaloblastoid changes, and occasional binucleated forms. Myelopoiesis was strongly suppressed with left shift, hypogranular neutrophils, and pseudo–Pelger-like forms. Plasma cells were slightly increased but morphologically unremarkable. No blast excess (<5%).
Flow cytometry demonstrated a very low cellularity sample with predominance of T-lymphocytes and an inverted CD4/CD8 ratio (0.6). A minimal residual population of myeloid precursors was present without aberrant antigen expression. No evidence of clonal B-lymphocytes, plasma cell dyscrasia, or blast population was detected.
Histology (biopsy) revealed hypocellular marrow spaces (cellularity <10%) largely replaced by adipose tissue. Sparse hematopoietic islands showed residual erythroid precursors with dysplasia, isolated myeloid elements, and almost no megakaryocytes. Small foci of reactive lymphoid infiltrates were present. Iron stain showed increased iron stores and occasional ring sideroblasts (<5%). Reticulin stain showed no marrow fibrosis (MF-0).
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "karyotype results and cytogenetic analysis"}}

Cytogenetics:
Preliminary cytogenetics: 46,XX[2] / 45,XX,-7[18].

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Severe aplastic anemia (SAA) with monosomy 7 (clonal evolution/MDS-related cytogenetics) in known PNH patient - high-risk for progression to MDS/AML", "differentials": ["Aplastic anemia with monosomy 7 - high-risk clonal evolution", "PNH-aplastic anemia syndrome with cytogenetic progression", "MDS-EB1 overlapping with aplastic picture", "Therapy-related cytopenias from immunosuppressants"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Urgent HLA typing of patient and siblings for allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) - monosomy 7 is an indication for early transplant", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Consider alternative donor search (matched unrelated donor, haploidentical) if sibling donor unavailable", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Continue filgrastim (G-CSF) daily for severe neutropenia", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Maintain current antifungal therapy (voriconazole) for residual fusariosis", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Reduce or discontinue potentially cytopenic medications (eltrombopag, ciclosporin) pending transplant evaluation", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Continue transfusion support (RBC weekly, platelets 3-4x/week)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Consider hypomethylating agent (azacitidine) as bridge to transplant if transplant delayed", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Monitor for progression to overt MDS/AML with repeat bone marrow in 3-4 months if no transplant", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Continue pegcetacoplan for PNH clone management pending transplant decision", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Severe aplastic anemia (SAA) with monosomy 7 (clonal evolution/MDS-related cytogenetics) in known PNH patient - high-risk for progression to MDS/AML
differentials: Aplastic anemia with monosomy 7 - high-risk clonal evolution
PNH-aplastic anemia syndrome with cytogenetic progression

treatment_plan.action: Urgent HLA typing of patient and siblings for allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) - monosomy 7 is an indication for early transplant
Consider alternative donor search (matched unrelated donor, haploidentical) if sibling donor unavailable
Continue filgrastim (G-CSF) daily for severe neutropenia
Maintain current antifungal therapy (voriconazole) for residual fusariosis
Reduce or discontinue potentially cytopenic medications (eltrombopag, ciclosporin) pending transplant evaluation
Consider hypomethylating agent (azacitidine) as bridge to transplant if transplant delayed
Monitor for progression to overt MDS/AML with repeat bone marrow in 3-4 months if no transplant
Continue pegcetacoplan for PNH clone management pending transplant decision

Round 3 begins.
Setting: High-risk marrow failure and MDS transplant-planning discussion in the context of invasive fungal disease
Capabilities: You have full information including NGS (TET2, DNMT3A), detailed PNH clone quantification, prior IST response, and the course of disseminated fusariosis with ocular and pulmonary involvement. You must define the final diagnosis, antifungal strategy, and an allogeneic SCT plan, balancing curative intent against infectious risk and the patient's reluctance toward prolonged hospitalization.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "NGS mutation results (TET2, DNMT3A) and PNH clone quantification data"}}

response.request: NGS mutation results (TET2, DNMT3A) and PNH clone quantification data

content:
Molecular Panel:
Mutations detected in TET2 (VAF 21%) and DNMT3A (VAF 12%). No pathogenic variants detected in TP53, RUNX1, ASXL1, IDH1, IDH2, FLT3, NPM1, SRSF2, U2AF1, ZRSR2, STAG2, CEBPA, MECOM.

solve.working_diagnosis: Severe aplastic anemia (SAA) with monosomy 7 (high-risk cytogenetic abnormality) in background of PNH - consistent with MDS-related clonal evolution. TET2 (21%) and DNMT3A (12%) mutations support clonal hematopoiesis with high risk of progression to MDS/AML
differentials: Aplastic anemia with monosomy 7 - high-risk MDS/AML evolution
PNH-aplastic anemia syndrome with cytogenetic progression

treatment_plan.action: Proceed immediately with HLA typing of patient, siblings, and parents; simultaneously initiate matched unrelated donor (MUD) search - monosomy 7 is definitive indication for urgent allogeneic HSCT
Continue voriconazole prophylaxis during transplant work-up; consider IV liposomal amphotericin B if fungal burden increases given persistent pulmonary nodules
Continue filgrastim (G-CSF) daily to maintain neutrophil count and reduce infection risk while awaiting transplant
Continue pegcetacoplan for PNH clone control - maintain complement inhibition through transplant
Continue current transfusion support (RBC weekly, platelets 3-4x/week) as bridge to transplant
Consider romiplostim addition for platelet support if eltrombopag dose reduced
Optimize antifungal coverage pre-transplant - proceed with CT-guided biopsy of pulmonary nodule to confirm Fusarium species and susceptibility testing
Refer to transplant center with expertise in fungal disease for evaluation of reduced-intensity conditioning (RIC) regimen appropriate for active invasive fungal infection
Psychosocial support and patient education about transplant necessity given high-risk cytogenetics - acknowledge patient's fear of hospitalization but emphasize curative intent
